title: Trim hot-tick audio reapply from standalone playback
summary: 0.6.2 keeps the clean 0.6.0 baseline and removes redundant per-frame audio-state reapply work from the standalone player runtime tick so playback does less hot-loop churn.
reasoning: The current player needs are audio sync, video sync, and general playback smoothness. The repo-local decision trace already records the stable seam here: standalone audio state belongs on explicit mutation paths such as load, mute, volume, and hosted audio refresh, not on the always-on playback loop.

changes.reasoning: This was previously documented as the stable overhead trim in the learning lane and is the cleanest bounded carry-forward into the 0.6.x baseline.

verification: Stamp version 0.6.2 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build the package-first player release from the 0.6.x baseline and confirm release validation passes.
Verify normal movie playback still has working audio, then compare broad playback feel against the 0.6.0 baseline for reduced churn.
knownIssues: This slice is intentionally narrow and does not yet change codec, decode, or seek-resume behavior.
The future dev package should include a packaged scene, but that packaging policy update is kept out of this playback-maintenance slice.
